title: Selling Stock Photos Without a Stripe Account: What Actually Works When You Are in a Restricted Country
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip do not work in your country. That is a platform problem, not a you problem. Here is what actually works.

content.text: Having a great stock photo to sell is one thing, but being unable to collect payment due to platform restrictions is a whole different story, especially when you are in a country like Nigeria where Stripe and PayPal are not available, leaving you wondering how to get paid for your digital products without these conventional payment processors, and this is where the search for alternatives begins, but what actually works and what does not, and how do you choose the right infrastructure for your digital store
What You Are Actually Comparing

When comparing different payment platforms, it is essential to understand the underlying mechanics that drive their exclusion of certain countries, which often boils down to compliance decisions, fraud risk tiers, and banking infrastructure limitations, and how these factors affect the ability of creators to sell their digital products and get paid, and it is crucial to find a platform that can bypass these restrictions and provide a seamless payment experience
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Traditional platforms like PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip often break down when it comes to serving creators in restricted countries, due to their strict compliance policies and limited banking infrastructure, which results in a significant number of creators being left without a reliable way to collect payments, and this is where the need for alternative payment solutions arises, and crypto-native infrastructure starts to look like a viable option
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, operates independently of traditional banking systems and geographic compliance layers, allowing creators to receive payments from anywhere in the world, without the need for a PayPal or Stripe account, and with the help of platforms like Unchained Commerce, creators can set up their own digital stores and start selling their products, using cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum, and get paid instantly
The ability to collect payments from anywhere in the world is a game-changer for creators in restricted countries, who often struggle to get paid for their digital products
The Real Cost Difference

The cost of using traditional platforms versus crypto-native infrastructure is also a significant factor to consider, as traditional platforms often charge high fees and require creators to pay for subscriptions and maintenance, whereas crypto-native infrastructure typically offers a one-time payment option, with no recurring fees, and this can make a huge difference for creators who are just starting out or have limited budgets, and are looking for a cost-effective solution
Which One to Use and Why

When it comes to choosing the right platform, creators should consider their specific needs and requirements, and evaluate the different options available, including traditional platforms and crypto-native infrastructure, and consider factors like fees, ease of use, and payment processing times, and for creators in restricted countries, crypto-native infrastructure is often the better choice, as it provides a reliable and cost-effective way to collect payments and sell digital products
What Creators in Restricted Countries Are Asking

For creators in Nigeria and other restricted countries, selling digital products without a PayPal or Stripe account can be a daunting task, but with the help of crypto-native infrastructure, it is possible to get paid online and sell stock photos without relying on traditional payment processors, and the best alternative to PayPal for creators in restricted countries is often a platform that uses cryptocurrencies and operates independently of traditional banking systems, and with platforms like Unchained Commerce, creators can set up their own digital stores and start selling their products, using cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum, and get paid instantly

Crypto-native infrastructure is not just a payment solution, but a platform independence that allows creators to take control of their digital stores and sell their products without relying on traditional platforms
Related Topics Worth Reading

Creators in restricted countries often face similar challenges when trying to sell their digital products, whether it is stock photos, ebooks, or courses, and the need for alternative payment solutions is a common thread that runs through all these scenarios, and by using crypto-native infrastructure, creators can break free from the limitations of traditional platforms and start selling their products to a global audience, without worrying about payment restrictions or high fees
Your Launch Checklist — No Platform Permission Required

Before launching your digital store, make sure you have a solid understanding of the payment infrastructure you will be using, and consider platforms like Unchained Commerce that offer a Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, which can provide a seamless payment experience and help you manage your store, and also make sure you have a clear understanding of the fees and payment processing times, and have a plan in place for handling customer support and refunds, and with the right infrastructure in place, you can focus on creating and selling your digital products, and get paid instantly

Success for creators in restricted countries is not just about making sales, but about being able to get paid instantly and reliably, and having the freedom to create and sell their digital products without worrying about payment restrictions
